response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 20+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web and TypeScript/Node.js development, which does not align with the deep C++ expertise this role requires. His only professional C++ work was a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a bachelor thesis project — there is no evidence of modern C++ (C++11/14/17/20), STL usage, multi-threaded systems, or any industrial-domain work. Critical requirements such as IEC 62443 secure development, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems, and industrial automation context are entirely absent from his profile.
strengths: Broad, multi-decade software engineering experience across domains demonstrates strong adaptability and technical range
Demonstrated event-driven architecture and REST API development experience that partially maps to IoT communication patterns
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, mentoring — aligns with the role's quality and team leadership expectations
concerns: C++ experience is effectively a single brief role from 2007–2008 with no evidence of modern C++, STL, concurrent programming, or high-performance systems work
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or any relevant industrial protocol (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
Primary technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is web-oriented and only tangentially relevant — the advantageous skills, not the core competencies

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 experience
